
=== SLIDE 1 ===
Unsupervised Concept Discovery
for Interpretable Clinical AI
CS4700 Dissertation Viva  ·  Visual + Lab Tabular Domains
Bhoopesh Nandan Singh
NHANES 2013–18  ·  18,673 Records
DINO ViT-S/8  ·  Oxford Pets  ·  CUB
GMM Clusters  ·  LLM Concept Labeling

=== SLIDE 2 ===
The Challenge: Interpretability Gap in Clinical AI
Black-box models cannot be safely deployed in clinical settings without auditability
Why Existing Approaches Fall Short
Clinicians cannot verify why a prediction was made — critical in high-stakes decisions
GDPR Article 22 &amp; EU AI Act require explanations for automated patient decisions
TCAV, CBM, ACE all require concept labels defined BEFORE or DURING training
Clinical concept annotation is expensive and rarely available at training time
Research Question
&quot;Can we discover clinically meaningful concepts from unlabeled data — and use them to make interpretable, auditable predictions?&quot;
Our Promise
Zero concept labels needed at training time
Semantically named, clinically coherent concepts
Fully auditable prediction paths per patient

=== SLIDE 3 ===
Three Literature Gaps This Work Addresses
Each gap maps directly to one of three design pillars — motivation is literature-grounded
Gap 1
Paper(s):
TCAV (Kim, ICML 2018)
CBM (Koh, ICML 2020)
ACE (Ghassemi, 2021)
Their Limitation:
Concept labels required before / during training. Pre-defined segmentation assumed.
Our Response:
GMM clustering discovers concepts unsupervised — labels applied post-hoc only. Zero annotation at training time.
Gap 2
Paper(s):
DINO (Caron, NeurIPS 2021)
FT-Transformer (Gorishniy, NeurIPS 2021)
Their Limitation:
Intermediate layer representations never analyzed for concept content.
Our Response:
Multi-layer extraction: concat(L8,L10,L12) for image; concat(L2,L4,L6) for lab. First to show concept structure in intermediate layers.
Gap 3
Paper(s):
ClusterLLM (EMNLP 2023)
Lab-MAE (CHIL 2025)
Their Limitation:
LLM concept guidance restricted to textual/NLP data only.
Our Response:
LLM reads numerical deviation profiles of cluster centroids, suggests clinical labels. Human validates. Bridges statistics and semantics for numerical data.

=== SLIDE 4 ===
Our Solution: A Three-Stage Unsupervised-First Pipeline
The identical structure applied to both domains: Visual (DINO) and Tabular/Lab (PSFTT)
Stage 1
DISCOVER
Encoder extracts features from
unlabeled data · GMM finds
30 natural clusters
Stage 2
NAME
LLM reads cluster centroid
deviation profiles · Human
validates clinical labels
Stage 3
PREDICT
Concept scores become
interpretable features · Classifier
gives auditable predictions
No supervision at any stage
Zero annotation at training time
Named, auditable predictions
Domain A — Visual:
DINO ViT-S/8  ·  Oxford Pets / Stanford Cars / CUB-200  ·  14,000 images  ·  3 classes
Domain B — Lab:
PSFTT + Panel PE  ·  NHANES 2013–2018  ·  18,673 records  ·  34 lab tests  ·  3 panels (CBC / BMP / Lipid)

=== SLIDE 5 ===
Domain A: Visual Pipeline — Primary Contribution
Architecture designed, implemented, and ablated here · 14,000 images · Oxford Pets / Stanford Cars / CUB-200
Image
Input
14k images
3 classes
DINO
ViT-S/8
Patch features
L8+L10+L12
PCA
+ GMM
1152→128 dim
30 clusters
Human
SME
label_tool.py
post-hoc naming
OneClass
SVM
Concept scores
→ classification
How Visual Concepts Are Named
GMM finds 30 clusters of visually similar patch-feature vectors
Human expert opens Streamlit label_tool.py — views thumbnail grid per cluster
Assigns concept name, e.g. Cluster 7 → &apos;cat_pointy_ear&apos;, Cluster 12 → &apos;car_wheel_rim&apos;
Concept score for an image = how strongly its patches activate each cluster
Key Design Innovation (Gap 2)
Multi-layer concat: L8 (texture) + L10 (part-shape) + L12 (semantics) = 1152-dim
Single-layer baseline loses 30%+ cluster purity — ablation B confirms this
Attention-guided FG masking removes background noise before GMM (ablation C)
Semantic fine-tuning (ablation D) further sharpens part-level cluster separation

=== SLIDE 6 ===
Visual Pipeline Output: Interpretable Prediction in Action
Every prediction traced to named concepts — no black-box, no post-hoc rationalisation
Top concepts:
bird:eye (0.12)  ·  bird:neck (0.12)  ·  bird:tail (0.12)  ·  bird:wings (0.11)  ·  bird:feather (0.07)
Cat &amp; car concepts: all near-zero  ·
Concepts found with zero class-label supervision — named post-hoc by human SME via label_tool.py

=== SLIDE 7 ===
Concept Specificity Confirmed Across All Three Classes
Correct-class concepts dominate every prediction · Cross-class concepts register near-zero · Zero supervision at concept-learning stage
CAT
→   cat:face · cat:nose · cat:eye
CAR
→   car:bumpe corner · car:side fender · car:wheel arch
BIRD
→   bird:eye · bird:neck · bird:tail · bird:wings

=== SLIDE 8 ===
Domain B: Lab Pipeline — Generalisation Study
Same 3-stage pipeline applied to tabular clinical data · PSFTT replaces DINO · LLM replaces human SME
NHANES
Data
18,673 records
34 lab tests
PSFTT
Encoder
Panel PE + SSL
L2+L4+L6 layers
Per-Panel
GMM
3 panels × 10
= 30 concepts
LLM
Labeling
Deviation profile
→ clinical name
LogReg
Probe
30 concept scores
→ diagnosis
LLM-Mediated Grounding (Gap 3)
GMM centroid = mean value of all 34 tests for records in that cluster
Deviation computed: (centroid − population_mean) / std per test
LLM receives: &apos;CBC cluster 4: LDL +2.1σ, cholesterol +1.8σ, HDL −0.3σ...&apos;
LLM suggests label + confidence; human clinician validates or corrects
PSFTT: Panel-Structured FT-Transformer
Custom tokenizer: each lab test has its own weight vector  Tᵢ = xᵢ·Wᵢ + bᵢ
Panel Positional Encoding: CBC=0, BMP=1, Lipid=2 — encodes clinical domain knowledge
Two-scale SSL: within-panel masking + cross-panel dropout (addresses Lab-MAE gap)
Per-panel embeddings: [N × 3 × 384-dim] fed to per-panel GMM independently

=== SLIDE 9 ===
All 30 Discovered Lab Concepts Are Clinically Coherent
Named by LLM from numerical deviation profiles only — zero diagnosis labels used at any stage
30/30
concepts validated as clinically plausible   ·
1
&apos;healthy_population&apos; cluster spontaneously discovered (Lipid panel, n=1,221)
CBC Panel
hypercholesterolemia_pattern
hypercholesterolemia
dyslipidaemia
pre_diabetes
metabolic_syndrome
hyperlipidemia_pattern
dyslipidemia_profile
elevated_lipid_pattern
BMP / Biochem Panel
metabolic_syndrome
mild_metabolic_syndrome
postprandial_hyperglycemia
dyslipidaemia_pattern
pre_diabetes_pattern
hyperlipidemia_pattern
elevated_ldl_pattern
dyslipidemia
Lipid Panel
healthy_population  ✓
hypercholesterolemia_pattern
metabolic_syndrome_pattern
pre_dyslipidaemia_pattern
mildly elevated CVD risk
hyperlipidemia_pattern
mild_hyperglycemia
metabolic_syndrome

=== SLIDE 10 ===
Classification Results: Concept Scores Carry Diagnostic Signal
LogReg probe on 30 concept scores — no diagnosis labels used during concept learning
Classification accuracy (%)
Per-Class Performance (Test Set)
Class
Recall
F1-score
diabetes
0.05
0.09
hypertension
0.01
0.02
normal
0.98
0.67
Interpretation
50.9% = 1.53× chance — unsupervised concepts carry real diagnostic signal
Classifier collapses to &apos;normal&apos; — GMM optimises cluster quality, not decision boundaries
Trade-off is explicit: lower accuracy vs. a fully named, auditable concept trail per patient

=== SLIDE 11 ===
Design Decisions: Three Encoder Innovations Validated by Ablation
Each decision directly addresses one literature gap and is tested against a controlled baseline
Multi-Layer Extraction
Gap 2
Our Choice:
Concat L2+L4+L6 (lab) / L8+L10+L12 (image)
Alternative:
Single final layer only
Why:
Each layer encodes different abstraction level: syntax → local pattern → semantic structure
Ablation:
Ablation: single-layer → silhouette score ↓, NMI ↓, class purity ↓
Panel Positional Encoding
Domain-specific
Our Choice:
Explicit tokens: CBC=0, BMP=1, Lipid=2
Alternative:
Learned PE or no PE
Why:
Encodes clinical knowledge: panel membership is clinically meaningful, not arbitrary
Ablation:
Ablation: learned PE → inter-panel concept blending increases, per-panel purity ↓
Two-Scale SSL Masking
Lab-MAE gap
Our Choice:
Within-panel masking + cross-panel dropout
Alternative:
Lab-MAE random masking
Why:
Learns local panel biochemistry AND cross-panel correlations simultaneously
Ablation:
Ablation: random masking only → NMI drops across all 3 panels

=== SLIDE 12 ===
Core Contributions: Three Pillars, Three Gaps Closed
This work advances interpretable ML for clinical tabular and visual data simultaneously
Pillar 1
Unsupervised-First
Concept Discovery
Closes Gap 1 — TCAV / CBM / ACE
GMM clustering on encoder features — zero concept labels at any stage
Concepts emerge from data structure, named post-hoc only
Demonstrated on image AND tabular clinical domains
Pillar 2
Domain-Informed
Encoder Design
Closes Gap 2 — DINO / FT-Transformer
Multi-layer feature extraction produces richer concept representations
Panel PE encodes clinical domain knowledge directly into architecture
Two-scale SSL learns local + global clinical biochemical patterns
Pillar 3
LLM-Mediated
Semantic Grounding
Closes Gap 3 — ClusterLLM
First application of LLM concept grounding to numerical clinical data
LLM reads deviation profiles → clinical label → human validates
Result: 30 clinically plausible concept names, zero supervision

=== SLIDE 13 ===
Conclusion &amp; Future Work
What Was Achieved
Two-domain pipeline with shared 3-stage architecture (Visual + Lab)
30 clinically coherent concepts discovered from NHANES without any supervision
LLM grounding of numerical data — novel extension beyond NLP (Gap 3)
Concept scores achieve 1.53× chance accuracy with fully auditable predictions
Ablations confirm each of the three design decisions against controlled baselines
Future Work
Extend to MIMIC-IV for physician-confirmed diagnosis codes at scale
Non-linear concept classifiers (kernel SVM, MLP) to improve disease-class recall
Weighted GMM sampling to reduce majority-class collapse in concept space
Concept drift detection: alert when patient patterns fall outside known clusters
Formal EU AI Act Article 13 compliance audit for clinical deployment

=== SLIDE 14 ===

Questions &amp; Discussion
